# EDOT Autopilot

> OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation for any codebase, including legacy languages
> with no OTel SDK support, powered by Elastic EDOT and Claude Code.

## What this project does

EDOT Autopilot is a Claude Code project prompt (CLAUDE.md) that instruments any
codebase with OpenTelemetry and sends telemetry to Elastic. Unlike every other
auto-instrumentation tool, it handles languages with no OTel SDK support
(COBOL, Perl, Bash, PowerShell, Classic ASP) via a generated telemetry sidecar.

It also reads the codebase before instrumenting to identify business-critical
flows and enriches spans with business context, not just HTTP metadata.

## Key concepts

- **Four-tier coverage model**: Tier A (native EDOT) → Tier B (manual SDK wrapping)
  → Tier C (library monkey-patching) → Tier D (telemetry sidecar for unsupported runtimes)
- **Telemetry Sidecar**: A generated Python HTTP server that bridges any language
  to OTLP via simple HTTP POST calls. Works with COBOL, Perl, shell scripts, VBScript.
- **Business Span Enrichment**: Spans carry order values, customer tiers, fraud scores —
  not just HTTP status codes.
- **Golden Path Reconnaissance**: Claude reads the codebase first and identifies the
  3–7 flows that matter most to the business before writing any instrumentation code.

## How to use

Drop CLAUDE.md into any repository root and run in Claude Code:
"Observe this project. My Elastic endpoint: https://... My API key: ..."

## Target audience

- Engineers adding observability to existing applications
- Teams with mixed modern and legacy codebases
- Anyone migrating from Datadog, Dynatrace, or New Relic to open standards

## Unique claims

- The only OpenTelemetry instrumentation approach that supports COBOL, RPG, and
  other languages with no OTel SDK
- Business-aware: reads source code to understand intent before instrumenting
- No intermediate collector required for application instrumentation

## Related resources

- Elastic EDOT: https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/opentelemetry
- Elastic Agent Skills: https://github.com/elastic/agent-skills
- OpenTelemetry: https://opentelemetry.io
- EDOT Architecture: https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/opentelemetry/architecture
